title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · API Object Lens: K8s Extension Interface Boundaries
summary: A cluster extension stores an underspecified custom resource, mutates workloads through an unavailable webhook, and upgrades without conversion or rollback evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s extension interface boundaries increment in trusted extension API and controller contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish aggregated APIs, CRDs, admission, scheduler plugins, device resource APIs, CNI, CSI, CRI, and Gateway implementations by authority and conformance.
Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Extension Interface Boundaries · Theory K8s Extension Interface Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Extension Interface Boundaries contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Distinguish aggregated APIs, CRDs, admission, scheduler plugins, device resource APIs, CNI, CSI, CRI, and Gateway implementations by authority and conformance. A defensible solution applies K8s Extension Interface Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trusted extension API and controller contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-extension-interface-boundaries — Distinguish aggregated APIs, CRDs, admission, scheduler plugins, device resource APIs, CNI, CSI, CRI, and Gateway implementations by authority and conformance.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-3
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: worked k8s extensions admission operators 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-extension-interface-boundaries-8v628l
  - competency: k8s-extension-interface-boundaries
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: extension interface boundaries
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: fill ephemeral storage before graceful termination
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: 8v628l
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
